steps.title: Set up the perimeter equation
steps.content: The perimeter of a triangle equals the sum of its three side lengths. Two sides measure $5$ cm and $7$ cm, and the perimeter is $16$ cm. Let $x$ represent the length of the third side:$$5 + 7 + x = 16$$

steps.title: Solve for x
steps.content:
$$\begin{align*}5 + 7 + x &= 16 \\ 12 + x &= 16 \\ x &= 4\end{align*}$$
The third side has length $4$ cm. That matches choice B — B is correct.
